This performance will include selections from different eras and styles, along with brief introductions to provide an overview of the musical journey. events.title: St. Lawrence Library Relocation and Expansion (Children's Services & Programming Focus Group)
events.description: Toronto Public Library is preparing plans to relocate and expand the St. Lawrence Branch. The new district branch at 339 Queen Street East will offer expanded space and services - and we’d like your input. Share your ideas on the features and services you’d like to see in the new location. This session will explore children’s services and programming for the new branch.

The audience and author will come up with words to base a story on, then orally tell a story from their new collection of words. Participants will be invited to then create their own story. ​This creative writing workshop is for children 3- 8.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Builders Club
events.description: Do you like to build with LEGO, DUPLO, Feltro, Snap Circuits or KEVA? Join us every Saturday morning for some fun building challenges! All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Asian Heritage Month Storytime
events.description: Join us for a special storytime program to celebrate Asian Heritage Month. This interactive session features captivating stories and tales from Asia, followed by hands-on origami craft where kids can create paper cranes, flowers, or lanterns. All ages are welcome.

events.title: Fay & Fluffy's Story Time
events.description: Join Fay & Fluffy (stars of Family Jr’s The Fabulous Show with Fay & Fluffy) as they share some of their favourite books and stories. Fay & Fluffy’s main goal is to share the message of inclusivity wherever they go, cheering on all the kids who see them be proud of who they are, loving themselves, and being kind to one another. Drop-in. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Fire Fighter Storytime
events.description: Join us for this fun story time to learn about fire safety and prevention, and what to do if there's an emergency. You will meet a Toronto Fire Fighter! At the end of the story time, you'll create a craft to take home. For children ages 0-5+ with a parent or caregiver. Drop in.  No Registration required.

events.title: Kamishibai: Storytelling & Craft
events.description: Join us for traditional Japanese Kamishibai storytelling told with illustrated cards, followed by a fun craft activity. For all ages! DROP-IN!

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: 3D Design with Tinkercad for Kids (Ages 9+)
events.description: Learn the basics of 3D design using a browser-based program called Tinkercad. You'll learn how to navigate in the 3D environment and create a simple object. Basic mouse and keyboarding skills are required. This course is designed for ages 9 - 14. R egistration is required.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve.

events.title: Saturday Family Club
events.description: Come celebrate your Saturday morning at Port Union Library! You can colour your favorite characters or create freestyle Legos with family and friends!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot. No registration required.

events.title: Girls Coding
events.description: This program introduces children to beginner computer programming and fosters the development of essential STEM skills. Delivered in collaboration with Macaulay Centres for Children, the initiative focuses on promoting educational growth and enhancing future employment opportunities, particularly for children in marginalized communities. Participants will engage in hands-on coding activities, make new friends, and have fun in a supportive. Space is limited, and advance registration with a commit

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Saturday STEAM Club
events.description: Join us for Saturday STEAM Club for fun-filled Arts or STEM programs. Come explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through hands-on activities and creative projects. It's a fun, interactive way to learn and discover new skills in a supportive environment! Younger children, accompanied by a caregiver, are welcome if space permits. Program will run once every month.

events.title: Art Journaling
events.description: Join us for an hour of creative art journaling fun! An art journal can include writing, drawing, painting, collage, stickers and more! No experience necessary, all materials will be provided. This is a drop-in program for all ages, no registration required.

events.title: Story Time and Craft
events.description: Join us on Saturday afternoons at Kennedy/Eglinton for a Story Time and Craft. We will read stories, sing songs, do a craft and have fun! All children and their caregivers are welcome.

events.title: Ice cream cone stamp
events.description: Come celebrate the beginning of summer with us by making your very own ice cream stamps! events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing a

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL Digital Innovation Hubs.

events.title: Mimico Kids & Parents Book Club
events.description: Our book for May is Grimm's Fairy Tales by Jakob & Wilhelm Grimm. First published in 1812, these timeless stories have enchanted generations of children. Some of the stories are scary, some whimsical, but all are spellbinding and have had a huge influence down through the years. These stories first introduced characters such as Hansel and Gretel, Rapunzel, Tom Thumb, and many more. Copies available at the library. Grimm's Fairy Tales

events.title: Sew Cool Kids:  Cross-Stitch
events.description: This fun and creative 4 week program introduces children aged 6 - 12 to the art of cross-stitching! Kids will learn the basic stitches, how to follow simple patterns, and how to use colorful threads to bring a design to life.  By the end of the program, each child will have completed a bright and cheerful stitched picture to proudly take home and display. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

We'll learn how to build simple circuits to power lights, make sounds, and more. Participants will work in pairs to construct projects from the Snap Circuits blueprints. No prior experience is needed—just bring your curiosity! Drop in. No registration required, but space is limited.

events.title: Lego Free Play

There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

For school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program.

Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.description: Ages 9-12, participants must attend all 4 sessions. events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Minute-to-Win-it!
events.description: For children ages 6-12. Compete in a series of challenges to earn points for your team. However, you only have one minute to complete each challenge! How steady are your hands and how fast can you think?  No registration – but ticket is required. Space is limited. Large groups and daycares cannot be accommodated. Tickets will be handed out at the 2nd floor desk 15 minutes before the program starts.

events.title: Crafternoon: Silhouette Chalk
events.description: Every second Friday make a fun craft! July 10 th : Makedo – Make a cool sculpture with the cardboard cutting tool Makedo, take a picture of it, then disassemble to build something else July 24 th : Bubble Painting – blow some bubbles to make some art August 7 th : Button Making – make your own design or colour a pin August 21 st : Silhouette Chalk – rub some chalk to show the image Children ages 4-12 and their parents or caregivers for support.

events.title: Mermaid Tails
events.description: Create a mermaid tail and explore various patterns using a variety of tactile decorative items. events.title: Creative Cardboard Construction with Makedo
events.description: Create, build, and invent with Makedo cardboard construction tools! Kids will use recycled cardboard and safe building tools to design unique structures and imaginative creations while exploring creativity and basic engineering concepts.

events.title: Perler Bead Craft
events.description: Get creative and design your own perler beads craft to take home. Materials will be provided. Perfect for kids ages 6-12.

events.title: A Very Gru-Some Summer Movie Series
events.description: Experience the hit movies featuring supervillain turned secret agent, Gru and his group of colourful little minions this summer in our library's theatre! Movie will play at 2pm sharp! July 3 - Despicable Me. (2010) 95 min. Rated G  July 17 - Despicable Me 2. (2013) 98 min. Rated G July 31 - Despicable Me 3. (2017) 90 min. Rated G August 7 - Minions. (2015) 91 min. Rated G August 21 - Minions: The Rise of Gru. (2022) 88 min. Rated G Drop-in. No registration needed.

events.title: DJ At Any Age: Intro to DJing for Kids
events.description: For ages 10 to 12.  Registration opens July 15.   Learn basic DJing skills in this hands-on camp with DJ At Any Age. Use a DJ controller and Serato DJ Lite software to play songs, add effects and have fun with samples. Show off your skills in a DJ Recital for your friends and family. events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers.

events.title: Builders Club
events.description: Do you like to build with LEGO, DUPLO, Feltro, Snap Circuits or KEVA? Join us every Saturday morning for some fun building challenges! All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Princess Storytime
events.description: Wearing her crown, sash and gown, Rachel will inspire and encourage everyone to be whatever they choose to be, dream big, and to strive to achieve their goals. She will read stories, encourage literacy, and share her own love of reading. To add to the fun, the audience is welcome to come dressed as princesses or in other costumes of what they want to be. Tickets will be given out one hour before the event.

events.title: Water Colour Fish Painting
events.description: Join the Summer Reading Club and explore the theme of Dive In! Learn the basics of water colour painting by learning to paint a fishy friend. No prior experience is required but it is a good idea to dress for mess, think short sleeves or nothing white that will show a stain.

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Community Challenge
events.description: What do you see when you walk through your community? Have you ever thought about building something for your community that would have a positive impact on the people living there? Put on your thinking cap and use your imagination to design and build something that would make your community stronger! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids age 6-12.Registration Required. Space is limited.

This fun, hands-on class will inspire imagination while teaching techniques that bring the underwater world to life with depth and texture. events.title: After School Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to our After School Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! For ages [6-12]. Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: Game Time
events.description: Video games, board games, and STEM games. Try using the Sphero robots as a controller or make shapes with the Tangrams, the possibilities are endless. Hang out with friends or meet new friends as we play together. For children ages 4-12.

events.title: Perler Beads
events.description: For children ages 8-12. Unleash your inner artist and get creative using fuse beads, templates and your imagination to create perler bead art! All materials are provided. No registration – but ticket is required. Space is limited. Large groups and daycares cannot be accommodated. Tickets will be handed out at the 2nd floor desk 15 minutes before the program starts.

events.title: Arts & Crafts
events.description: Let your creativity loose at Cliffcrest Library with our Summer Wonder Arts & Crafts program! We'll be making different projects every week in July & August. All supplies will be provided and you get to take your creations home! Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Mixed Media Forest Adventure for Kids
events.description: Children will explore the beauty of the forest through a fun and imaginative mixed media art program inspired by forest animals and trees. Using a variety of materials such as paint, collage, textured paper, natural elements, and drawing tools, kids will create unique artworks while learning about woodland creatures and nature This is a Drop-In program. No registration required. Space is limited. All materials are provided

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Youth Hub: Game Night
events.description: Join us every Friday and play your favourite games with your friends. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Games
events.description: Roll the dice and challenge your friends in some classic games. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play various video games with other teens. The video game consoles that are provided are the PS5 and Nintendo Switch. Drop-in program, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Open Mic at Toronto Reference Library (Music, Poetry & Storytelling)
events.description: Are you a musician, poet, or storyteller? If so, then you are invited to share your talents at the Toronto Reference Library Open Mic! Please note: stand-up comedy is no longer featured at this event. Guitar and piano provided. 5 minutes for each performance. Program is for adults, teens, and seniors. Sign-up is at 5:30 pm. For more information, please contact the Arts Department at 416-393-7157. ** Accessibility at Toronto Public Library: Toronto Public Library is committed to accessibilit

events.title: Open Mic at the Library!
events.description: Are you a musician, poet, or storyteller? If so, then you are invited to share your talents at the Parkdale Library Open Mic! Please note: stand-up comedy is not featured at this event. Guitar, ukulele, keyboard, and more instruments provided. 5 minutes for each performance. Program is for adults, teens, and seniors. Sign-up will take place at 6:00 pm.

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others! This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot. No registration required.

events.title: Kamishibai: Storytelling & Craft
events.description: Join us for traditional Japanese Kamishibai storytelling told with illustrated cards, followed by a fun craft activity. For all ages! DROP-IN!

events.title: FRESH Movie Saturdays
events.description: This program helps people stay active, make friends, learn new skills, and have fun. Meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighbourhood! Come join us every Saturday for screenings of your favourite movies. In addition to this, enjoy cuddles with a therapy dog! Therapy dog Goose visits Fort York Branch on alternate Saturdays from 11:00AM-12:00PM. We'll be showing the following films: May 9-Stuart Little May 16-The Jungle

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

You are also welcome to bring your own and invite others to play with you. Children must be accompanied by an adult.

events.title: Let's Learn About Computer Hardware
events.description: This hands-on workshop series introduces the process of building a gaming desktop, from selecting components to installing an operating system. Participants will disassemble a desktop to learn about internal hardware and basic troubleshooting. The series also covers OS selection and installation, along with essential cybersecurity tips using open-source tools. This workshop is run by Free Geek Toronto.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: 3D Fundamentals with Blender Part III: 3D Animation
events.description: In this part of the Blender 3D Fundamentals Series, participants will learn how to use and understand 3D animation tools such as armatures, keyframes, the dopesheet and graph editor to bring their 3D model to life. Registration is required. Computers will provided for this class. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). The information will only be used for eve

events.title: Employment Summer Workshop
events.description: The Canadian Training Institute is thrilled to present a 2 part workshop that will introduce participants to employment and job search skills for future success Topics will include: Job search anxiety and building resilience Resume and mindset Interview preparation and presentation Understanding and leveraging learning styles for job success Volunteer hours can be provided to participants upon completion of the workshop.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video/Board Games

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Classic Tiramisu Workshop
events.description: Learn the basics of how to make the classic Italian dessert, Tiramisu. Chef Yumiko will provide and explain all the ingredients and take the participants through a simple step-by-step process of creating a delicious ready-to-eat dish. *Food allergy notice: Please be aware that ingredients used in this program include dairy, egg and decaffeinated espresso. Certain food ingredients may cause allergic reactions in some people. Registration is required. Information Collection Notice: The personal in

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) June 4: Crazy Stupid Love (2001, PG) June 11: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014, 14A) June 18: Best in Show (2000, PG) June 25: The Big Lebowski (1998, 14A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop with Chef Yumiko for Teens
events.description: In this 45 minute to 1 hour workshop (depending on the level of engagement), participants will learn how to prepare a classic milk bubble tea, including decorating and mixing techniques, as well as the breakdown of core ingredients. A classic lidded cup and straw will be provided to enjoy the boba as you go! Food allergy notice: Please be aware that food will be used in this program. Certain food ingredients may cause allergic reactions in some people.  (*contains dairy, sugar, and caffeine

events.title: 3D Design with Autodesk Fusion
events.description: A hands-on workshop where participants will become familiar with working in a 3D environment by creating simple 3D objects using Autodesk Fusion software. It is strongly recommended that you create an account at autodesk.ca using a valid e-mail address.  Note: users of the Tinkercad platform might already possess one. Registration is required.  Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act,

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: Screenwriting for Beginners
events.description: Have an idea for a movie, but not sure how to write it? Writing a screenplay can be tricky as the industry has strict rules on how they are formatted.This workshop will guide participants on how to properly format a screenplay and the different software available to make this process easier. Computers will be provided for this program. events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Art and Game Workshop
events.description: This program is part of Youth service in a welcoming place for teens in the summer.  Do you love Games and crafts? events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. It is designed to help youth understand workplace expectations and succeed in their first roles. For teen sessions, bringing a resume is optional. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available.&nbs

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.
